annotations.label: Angle

anchor.snippet: an angle is formed by two lines that meet at a point

annotations.note: EuclideanGeometry.angle (∠ p₁ p₂ p₃) is the undirected angle at a vertex p₂ formed by two rays/lines meeting there.

annotations.label: Standard definition of angle (two rays)

anchor.snippet: an angle is a figure consisting of two rays which lie in a plane

annotations.note: EuclideanGeometry.angle is defined as the undirected angle at p₂ between the segments/rays to p₁ and p₃.

annotations.label: Angles from segments/curves

anchor.snippet: angles are also formed wherever two line segments come together

annotations.note: Angles between line segments meeting at a point are formalized as EuclideanGeometry.angle, but the curve/tangent case is not.

annotations.label: Interior and exterior of an angle

anchor.snippet: the interior of the angle and the exterior of the angle

annotations.note: No declaration defines the interior/exterior region of an angle as a point set.

annotations.label: Angle notation

anchor.snippet: is used to identify angles in geometric figures

annotations.note: Mathlib provides the ∠ notation (and ∡ for oriented) for the angle function, realizing the standard angle notation.

annotations.label: Signed angle convention

anchor.snippet: signed angles are used by convention to indicate a direction of rotation

annotations.note: Orientation.oangle is the oriented (signed) angle valued in Real.Angle = ℝ/2πℤ, encoding direction of rotation.

annotations.label: Full angle (complete rotation)

anchor.snippet: performs a compete rotation about the vertex

annotations.note: A full rotation 2π is identified with 0 in Real.Angle (coe_two_pi), but no standalone 'full angle' unit is defined.

annotations.label: Degrees and turns

anchor.snippet: Degrees and turns are defined directly with reference to a full angle

annotations.note: Mathlib measures angles in radians (real numbers) and has no degree or turn units.

annotations.label: Radian

anchor.snippet: Radians are not defined directly in relation to a full angle

annotations.note: Angles are real-valued in radians by convention, but the radian as a defined unit (arc/radius) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Angle addition postulate
annotations.note: angle_add_of_ne_of_ne proves ∠ b a p + ∠ p a c = ∠ b a c when p lies between b and c (Wbtw).

anchor.snippet: The angle addition postulate states that if D is a point lying in the interior

annotations.label: Zero angle

anchor.snippet: is called a zero angle

annotations.note: The value 0 and its characterization (angle_eq_zero_iff) exist, but 'zero angle' is not a named concept.

annotations.label: Acute angle

anchor.snippet: is called an acute angle

annotations.note: Acuteness (angle < π/2) is defined only as the AcuteAngled property of simplices, not as a standalone acute-angle predicate.

annotations.label: Right angle

anchor.snippet: is called a right angle

annotations.note: A right angle is the value π/2, characterized by orthogonality (inner_eq_zero_iff_angle_eq_pi_div_two), but not defined as a named concept.

annotations.label: Obtuse angle

anchor.snippet: is called an obtuse angle

annotations.note: There is no obtuse-angle predicate (angle > π/2) defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Straight angle

anchor.snippet: is called a straight angle

annotations.note: A straight angle is the value π, characterized by collinearity (angle_eq_pi_iff_sbtw), but not a named concept.

annotations.label: Reflex angle

anchor.snippet: is called a reflex angle

annotations.note: Unoriented angles range in [0,π] and there is no reflex-angle (π to 2π) concept defined.

annotations.label: Full angle / perigon

anchor.snippet: is called a full angle , complete angle , round angle or perigon

annotations.note: The value 2π corresponds to 0 in Real.Angle, but no 'perigon'/full-angle named concept exists.

annotations.label: Oblique angle

anchor.snippet: is called an oblique angle

annotations.note: No predicate for oblique angles (not a multiple of a right angle) is defined.

annotations.label: Adjacent angles

anchor.snippet: Adjacent angles (abbreviated adj. ∠s ), are angles that share a common vertex and edge

annotations.note: No notion of adjacent angles sharing a vertex and edge is defined.

annotations.label: Vertical angles

anchor.snippet: Vertical angles are formed when two straight lines intersect at a point

annotations.note: Vertically opposite angles appear in Sbtw.oangle_eq_left_right but are not defined as a standalone concept.

annotations.label: Vertical angles are congruent

anchor.snippet: A theorem states that vertical angles are always congruent or equal to each other

annotations.note: Sbtw.oangle_eq_left_right proves ∡ p₁ p₂ p₃ = ∡ p₁' p₂ p₃' when both pairs are on opposite rays (vertically opposite).

annotations.label: Transversal

anchor.snippet: A transversal is a line that intersects a pair of (often parallel) lines

annotations.note: No transversal-line concept is defined.

annotations.label: Complementary angles

anchor.snippet: Complementary angles are angle pairs whose measures sum to a right angle

annotations.note: No named concept of complementary angles (summing to π/2) is defined.

annotations.label: Supplementary angles

anchor.snippet: Supplementary angles sum to a straight angle

annotations.note: The supplementary relation (angles summing to π on a line) appears in angle_add_angle_eq_pi_of_angle_eq_pi but is not a named concept.

annotations.label: Explementary angles

anchor.snippet: Explementary angles or conjugate angles sum to a full angle

annotations.note: No concept of explementary/conjugate angles (summing to 2π) is defined.

annotations.label: Non-adjacent supplementary angles

anchor.snippet: Examples of non-adjacent supplementary angles include the consecutive angles of a parallelogram

annotations.note: No formalization of parallelogram consecutive-angle supplementarity.

annotations.label: Interior angle of a polygon

anchor.snippet: An angle that is part of a simple polygon is called an interior angle if it lies on the inside

annotations.note: Interior angles of triangles/simplices are expressed via EuclideanGeometry.angle at vertices, but general polygon interior angles are not defined.

annotations.label: Sum of interior angles

anchor.snippet: the measures of the interior angles of a triangle add up to

annotations.note: angle_add_angle_add_angle_eq_pi proves the three angles of a triangle sum to π; the general (n−2)π polygon case is not formalized.

annotations.label: Exterior angle

anchor.snippet: The supplement of an interior angle is called an exterior angle

annotations.note: The exterior-angle theorem for triangles is formalized (exterior_angle_eq_angle_add_angle), but 'exterior angle' is not a standalone definition.

annotations.label: Sum of exterior angles

anchor.snippet: the sum of the exterior angles of a simple convex polygon

annotations.note: The sum of exterior angles of a polygon (2π) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Exterior bisectors concurrent

anchor.snippet: the bisectors of two exterior angles and the bisector of the other interior angle are concurrent

annotations.note: No formalization of excenter/angle-bisector concurrency in these terms was found.

annotations.label: Collinear external bisector intersections

anchor.snippet: three intersection points, each of an external angle bisector with the opposite extended side , are collinear

annotations.note: This collinearity result for external bisectors is not formalized.

annotations.label: Collinear mixed bisector intersections

anchor.snippet: three intersection points, two between an interior angle bisector and the opposite side

annotations.note: This mixed-bisector collinearity result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Dihedral angle

anchor.snippet: is called a dihedral angle

annotations.note: Only the algebraic dihedral group exists; the geometric dihedral angle between half-planes is not defined.

annotations.label: Plane-line angle complementary to normal

anchor.snippet: The angle between a plane and an intersecting straight line is complementary

annotations.note: No angle-between-a-plane-and-line concept (complementary to the normal angle) is formalized.

annotations.label: Radian measure

anchor.snippet: is defined as an angle for which the ratio

annotations.note: The radian defined as arc-length-to-radius ratio is not formalized.

annotations.label: Angle measure independent of circle size

anchor.snippet: The value of θ thus defined is independent of the size of the circle

annotations.note: The scale-invariance of radian measure across circle sizes is not stated as a theorem.

annotations.label: Angle as dimensionless quantity

anchor.snippet: an angle is defined as a dimensionless quantity

annotations.note: Angles are plain real numbers; there is no dimensional-analysis framework treating them as dimensionless.

annotations.label: Angle in standard position

anchor.snippet: an angle is typically defined by its two sides, with its vertex at the origin

annotations.note: Oriented angles between vectors based at the origin are formalized, but 'standard position' with initial side along the positive x-axis is not a defined construct.

annotations.label: Equal / congruent angles

anchor.snippet: are said to be equal or congruent

annotations.note: Angle congruence is captured by equality of real-valued angle measures, not by a dedicated congruence relation.

annotations.label: Coterminal angles

anchor.snippet: Two angles that share terminal sides, but differ in size by an integer multiple of a turn, are called coterminal angles

annotations.note: Real.Angle = ℝ/2πℤ identifies coterminal angles, and angle_eq_iff_two_pi_dvd_sub states equality iff difference is an integer multiple of 2π.

annotations.label: Reference angle

anchor.snippet: The reference angle (sometimes called related angle ) for any angle

annotations.note: No reference-angle construction is defined.

annotations.label: Slope / gradient

anchor.snippet: The slope or gradient is equal to the tangent of the angle

annotations.note: slope is defined as a difference quotient, but its identity as the tangent of the inclination angle is not formalized.

annotations.label: Spread between lines

anchor.snippet: The spread between two lines is defined in rational geometry as the square of the sine

annotations.note: The rational-geometry 'spread' (sin² of the angle) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Angle between curves

anchor.snippet: The angle between a line and a curve (mixed angle) or between two intersecting curves

annotations.note: No notion of angle between curves via their tangents is defined.

annotations.label: Impossibility of trisection (Wantzel)

anchor.snippet: Pierre Wantzel showed that this construction could not be performed for most angles

annotations.note: Compass-and-straightedge constructibility and the impossibility of angle trisection are not formalized (only topological 'constructible sets' share the name).

annotations.label: Angle via dot product

anchor.snippet: is related to their dot product and their lengths by the formula

annotations.note: cos_angle states cos(angle x y) = ⟪x, y⟫ / (‖x‖ * ‖y‖), exactly the dot-product angle formula.

annotations.label: Angle in real inner product space

anchor.snippet: To define angles in an abstract real inner product space

annotations.note: InnerProductGeometry.angle is defined as arccos(⟪x,y⟫/(‖x‖‖y‖)) in an arbitrary real inner product space.

annotations.label: Angle in complex inner product space

anchor.snippet: In a complex inner product space , the expression for the cosine above may give non-real values

annotations.note: The angle definition is only over ℝ; no complex inner product space angle is defined.

annotations.label: Principal angles between subspaces

anchor.snippet: angles called canonical or principal angles between subspaces

annotations.note: Canonical/principal angles between subspaces are not formalized.

annotations.label: Angle in Riemannian geometry

anchor.snippet: the metric tensor is used to define the angle between two tangents

annotations.note: No definition of angle between tangent vectors via a Riemannian metric tensor was found.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic angle

anchor.snippet: A hyperbolic angle is an argument of a hyperbolic function

annotations.note: Hyperbolic functions (sinh/cosh/arsinh) exist, but the geometric hyperbolic angle (hyperbolic sector area) is not defined.

annotations.label: Euclid's definition of plane angle

anchor.snippet: Euclid defines a plane angle as the inclination to each other

annotations.note: The modern equivalent of Euclid's plane angle (inclination of two rays at a point) is EuclideanGeometry.angle.

annotations.label: Vertical angle theorem

anchor.snippet: The equality of vertically opposite angles is called the vertical angle theorem

annotations.note: Sbtw.oangle_eq_left_right is the vertical angle theorem: replacing both outer points by points on the opposite rays preserves the oriented angle.

annotations.label: Angular diameter of the full moon

anchor.snippet: the full moon has an angular diameter of approximately 0.5°

annotations.note: This is a numerical astronomy example with no formal counterpart in Mathlib.
